title: 7 Branch Capture Scanner Testing, Maintenance, and Troubleshooting
body_text:
7	Branch Capture Scanner Testing, Maintenance, and Troubleshooting
7.1	Accessing the Branch Capture Staging Environment
We recommend that you create a test user to test the scannerГўВҖВҷs functionality and to ensure that the computer, the scanner, and the Branch Capture application are interacting correctly. Branch Capture Users have access to Branch Capture in the staging environment and in production.
Note:	The staging environment is only available in Branch Capture.
A User Management Security Officer creates the test user in CPS Admin or in User Management, depending on the application youГўВҖВҷre using.
A Branch Capture test user must be set up as a ГўВҖВңCU Branch Deposit Clerk ГўВҖВ“ Capture UserГўВҖВқ. See Chapter 8, Branch Capture User Management, for more information.
To access the Branch Capture staging environment:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign In
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Selecting Applications on the Client Centre
Scroll to the bottom of the page and select
Access Test & Training Applications
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Accessing Test and Training Applications
Select
Branch Capture TEST
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Branch Capture TEST
Verify you are in the staging environment. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Verifying Staging Environment
7.2	Accessing the Scanner Diagnostic Tool
To access the scanner diagnostic tool:
Enter
http://localhost/pptcdrvrdiag
in the address bar of a web browser or go to the computerГўВҖВҷs start menu. Then select
All Programs
>
Passport Web Edition Client
>
WebClient Diagnostics
.
Use the tool to install or update your scanner driver or perform diagnostics on your scanner and the Branch Capture application.
7.3	Installing or Updating the Scanner Driver for Branch Capture
To process deposits in Branch Capture, you must install a scanner driver onto a designated computer. This is done by a test user or a user with existing access to Branch Capture. The user must also have Windows Administrator rights to the computer to install or update the scanner driver.
This section provides procedures for installing or updating and testing the scanner driver for Branch Capture. For related procedures in Corporate Capture, see Section 15.5, Installing, Updating, or Testing a Corporate Capture Scanner Driver, and Corporate Capture Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting.
To view the current driver:
Open the scanner diagnostic tool. See Section 7.2. Accessing the Scanner Diagnostic Tool.
View the scanner driver version on the NCR Passport Web Edition Client Diagnostic page. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: NCR Passport Web Edition Client Diagnostic Page
Important:
Turn the scanner power off and disconnect the USB cable from the computer before installing or updating the scanner driver.
To download and install or update the scanner driver:
1.
Log in to the Branch Capture test application as described in Section 7.1, Accessing the Branch Capture Staging Environment.
2.
Select ГўВҖВңPlease click
here
to download scanner driversГўВҖВқ in the Messages section on the Branch Capture home page. YouГўВҖВҷre redirected to the scanner driver download page.
3.
Select the scanner that most closely resembles your scanner model. See Figure 7. Save the installation file to the computer.
Figure 7: Driver Download Page
Right-click on the downloaded file, then select
Run as Administrator
to begin the driver installation wizard.
Select
Next
and follow the on-screen instructions. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Driver Installation Wizard
The installation wizard installs a new driver or updates your driver to the newer version. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Upgrading a Scanner Driver
Plug in and power on your scanner when prompted, then select
OK
to confirm. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Plug in and Power on Scanner Prompt
Confirm that the driver installation passes the requirements check, then select
Next
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Confirming Scanner Driver Installation
Select
Finish
to complete the driver installation.
See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Completing Scanner Driver Setup
7.4	Initializing and Testing the Scanner for Branch Capture
The scanner diagnostic tool allows users to initialize and test scanner connectivity and to generate driver logs that are used to analyze scanner issues. We recommend that you initialize a scanner prior to scanning items to ensure the scanner and computer are interacting properly. The computer, USB port, or scanner can go into standby mode if unused for a while.
This section provides procedures for initializing and testing the scanner driver for Branch Capture. For related procedures on Corporate Capture, see Section 15.5, Installing, Updating, or Testing a Corporate Capture Scanner Driver, and Corporate Capture Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting.
To initialize and test the scanner driver:
Open the scanner diagnostic tool. See Section 7.2, Accessing the Scanner Diagnostic Tool.
Select
Initialize Scanner
. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Initialize Scanner
Verify that the Scanner Initialization field displays ГўВҖВң
STATUS_SUCCESSГўВҖВқ
. Put a test cheque in the scanner hopper, then select
Get Next Cheque
. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Status Success
Verify that the Code Line MICR information read by the system matches the chequeГўВҖВҷs MICR. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Captured MICR
Note:	If one of the following page errors occurs, ГўВҖВңPage Cannot Be DisplayedГўВҖВқ or a ГўВҖВңLocal Host ErrorГўВҖВқ, confirm that Port 80 is available to the local host.
If one of the following scanner initialization messages displays, proceed as appropriate:
Status Ranger Timeout
ГўВҖВ“ Indicates a cheque wasnГўВҖВҷt loaded in the hopper. Put a cheque in the hopper, then select
Get Next Cheque
.
Deposit in Progress
ГўВҖВ“ Indicates an active deposit session is open. Delete the current deposit and initialize the scanner again.
Scanner_Transport Off
ГўВҖВ“ Indicates the scanner isnГўВҖВҷt interacting with the driver. Proceed as follows:
ГўВҲВ’
Verify the scanner power source is plugged in and the scanner is on.
ГўВҲВ’
Verify the USB cable is plugged into the scanner and an available (2.0) USB port on the computer.
ГўВҲВ’
Verify that you installed the Branch Capture driver before plugging the scanner into the computer. If the scanner is plugged into the computer before installing the Branch Capture driver, Windows default drivers will install, and you wonГўВҖВҷt be able to scan your deposits.
ГўВҲВ’
If you canГўВҖВҷt initialize the scanner, restart the computer.
See Section 7.11, Resolving a Scanner Transport Off Error, for more information.
7.5	Creating and Deleting a Test Deposit in the Branch Capture Staging Environment
To create a test deposit:
Log in to the Branch Capture Test Application as described in Section 7.1, Accessing the Branch Capture Staging Environment.
Select
Create New Deposit
. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Create New Deposit
Complete the New Deposit page as follows. See Figure 17.
ГўВҲВ’
Select a deposit type from the Account Number drop-down menu (you can select any deposit type for a deposit test).
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the test cheque amount in the ГўВҖВңDeclared amountГўВҖВқ field.
ГўВҲВ’
Place the test cheque in the scanner hopper, then select
Start Capture
.
Figure 17: New Deposit Page
Verify that the virtual credit and the scanned cheque details display on the Deposit Item List page. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Deposit Item List
Delete the deposit once youГўВҖВҷve finished reviewing it.
To delete the test deposit:
Select
Delete Deposit
on the Deposit Item List page, then select
OK
to confirm. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Delete Deposit
Close the browser and log out of the Client Centre.
7.6	Exporting and Saving Scanner Driver Logs in Branch Capture
If youГўВҖВҷre directed by support personnel to export the scanner driver logs:
Open the scanner diagnostic tool as described in Section 7.2, Accessing the Scanner Diagnostic Tool.
Select
Save Logs
. See Figure 20.
Figure 20: Save Logs
Record the
Log File Name
and where the zip file was saved on the computer.
Note: 	To save the file to a different location, right-click
Log File Name
. Depending on your browser, select
Save Target As
or
Save Link As
, then select the location to save the zip file.
Locate the scanner log zip file.
Follow the instructions from support personnel on where to send the log zip file for analysis.
7.7	Scanner Cleaning and Maintenance for Branch Capture
Dirt, ink, and dust in the scanner track and rollers affect image quality, scanner performance, and reliability. Regular scanner cleaning and maintenance is critical to capture clear and legible images. Failure to do so may result in scanner jams and/or illegible images that can be dishonoured by another financial institution.
We recommend cleaning your scanner weekly. Only use authorized cleaning supplies. Using unauthorized cleaning supplies may result in scanner damage, document damage, repair fees from your scanner service provider, and may void the scanner warranty. Refer to the scannerГўВҖВҷs user manual for maintenance instructions.
To use the Passport Web Edition Client cleaning cycle with an authorized scanner cleaning card:
Open the scanner diagnostic tool as described in Section 7.2, Accessing the Scanner Diagnostic Tool.
Put the cleaning card in the scanner hopper, then select
Clean Scanner
. See Figure 21. Follow your scannerГўВҖВҷs cleaning instructions.
Figure 21: Using the Passport Web Edition Client Cleaning Cycle
See Section 15.10, Scanner Maintenance and Troubleshooting for Corporate Capture, for information related to Corporate Capture.
7.8	Scanner Troubleshooting Tips
If the scanner wonГўВҖВҷt scan an item (due to, for example, a cheque misfeed or the cheque is creating a scanner jam):
Sort items in your deposit from small to large sizes. Put larger items behind the smaller items in the scanner hopper. Ensure items are in the same order as the tape listing.
Repair torn edges with clear tape. Ensure the leading and lower edges of the cheques are smooth. You can also use clear tape to smooth out wrinkles in a cheque that may cause MICR or amount misreads.
Fan items to separate them if the scanner model doesnГўВҖВҷt have a built-in jogger. Tap the bundle to align the leading edges together.
If an item doesnГўВҖВҷt feed through the scanner, scan the item on its own or scan the cheque between two other cheques that have clean edges.
If the scanner jams every time that you process a deposit, see Section 7.14 Resolving Scanner Jams, for troubleshooting procedures.
Additional Scanner Support
The following options are also available on the scanner diagnostic tool:
End Deposit Client Side for Diagnostics
ГўВҖВ“ Ends a capture session if youГўВҖВҷre unable to end the session in Branch Capture.
Shutdown Scanner
ГўВҖВ“ Shuts down the scanner and clears the driver processing state if you want to use a third-party scanner utility or other diagnostic application.
7.9	Resolving Canon Scanner Driver Issues after a Windows Operating System Upgrade
If your computer canГўВҖВҷt detect that your Canon scanner driver is connected after a Windows upgrade, have your local administrator replace the ГўВҖВңusbscan.sysГўВҖВқ file as follows:
Go to a working computer with a Windows 10 operating system and copy the ГўВҖВңusbscan.sysГўВҖВқ file from the following location: \windows\system\drivers.
Replace the ГўВҖВңusbscan.sysГўВҖВқ file on the computer thatГўВҖВҷs not detecting the scanner driver.
Install the driver on Device Manager. Search for the driver under ГўВҖВңC:\Program Files (x86)\NCR\Passport Web Edition\Ranger\RangerCore\Scanner Plug-ins\Scanner_Brand\DriverГўВҖВқ.
Reboot the computer and initialize the scanner.
7.10	Resolving an ГўВҖВңHTTP 12057 - SSL RevocationГўВҖВқ Error
To resolve an ГўВҖВңHTTP 12057 - SSL RevocationГўВҖВқ error, have your local administrator install a browser certificate as follows:
Go to the computerГўВҖВҷs start menu, then select
All Programs
>
Passport Web Edition Client
, then
Install Browser Certificate
.
Follow the on-screen instruction to install the browser certificate.
If you continue to get the error, update the browser certificate settings as follows:
Open Control Panel, select
Network and Internet
, then
Internet Options
.
Select
Advanced
, then deselect the following options under security settings:
ГўВҲВ’
ГўВҖВңCheck for publisherГўВҖВҷs certificate revocationГўВҖВқ
ГўВҲВ’
ГўВҖВңCheck for server certificate revocationГўВҖВқ
Restart the browser.
7.11	Resolving a Scanner Transport Off Error
A ГўВҖВңTransport Off ГўВҖВңerror indicates that the computer and the scanner arenГўВҖВҷt communicating. Verify that your scanner is on and connected to your computerГўВҖВҷs USB port. If necessary, restart your computer.
If, after checking the connections, your scanner continues to display the error, have your local administrator uninstall the scanner driver as described in Section 7.15, Replacing a Scanner with a New Scanner, and reinstall it as described in Section 7.3, Installing or Updating the Scanner Driver for Branch Capture.
If the uninstall and reinstall of the driver is unsuccessful:
Go to Device Manager in System Properties.
Update the driver path to ГўВҖВңC:\Program Files (x86)\NCR\Passport Web EditionГўВҖВқ.
Open the scanner diagnostics tool as described in Section 7.2, Accessing the Scanner Diagnostic Tool.
Ensure that Port 80 is available for the driver to use.
Plug the scanner into a different USB port on the computer.
Initialize the scanner as described in Section 7.4, Initializing and Testing the Scanner for Branch Capture. If you canГўВҖВҷt initialize the scanner, restart the computer.
7.12	Black Lines Appearing on Scanned Images
You must submit good quality cheque images for processing. Financial institutions can dishonour an item with poor image quality for up to 90 days after it clears. If single or multiple horizontal lines appear on images, the following are potential reasons why this may occur and how to resolve the issue:
Reason
Solution
There may be electrical interference from an appliance such as an oscillating fan, a space heater, an old-style CRT monitor, etc.
Ensure that any appliances are at least 18 inches away from the scanner.
The scanner may be plugged into a poor-quality electrical circuit.
Relocate the scannerГўВҖВҷs power source to another electrical outlet.
The USB controller or USB cable is degraded.
Replace the USB cable or use a powered USB hub for your scannerГўВҖВҷs USB connection to the computer.
Other USB devices are connected to the computer (e.g., laser or 3-in-one printer). These types of devices can sometimes cause interference with other USB devices attached to the computer.
Turn off the power to these devices to eliminate interference.
7.13	Reducing Piggybacks and Scanner Jams
If two or more items are imaged together through the scanner, itГўВҖВҷs called a piggyback. Piggybacks contain the front of one item with either the front or back of another behind it. Depending on how closely aligned items are when imaged, piggybacks may be obvious or subtle. When reviewing images, scrutinize them and ensure that there is only one item captured per image. See Section 9.9, Correcting Branch Capture Deposit Errors, for more information.
To reduce piggybacks:
Clean the scanner. See Section 7.7, Scanner Cleaning and Maintenance for Branch Capture, for procedures.
Note:	We recommend cleaning your scanner weekly to prevent poor quality images, scanner jams, and piggybacks. Follow the scanner manufacturerГўВҖВҷs cleaning and maintenance procedures.
Ensure items are fanned or jogged to remove static. Repair items with clear tape if necessary. Sort items by size and align the lower and leading edge of items before putting them in the scanner hopper.
To reduce scanner jams:
Clean the scanner. See Section 7.7, Scanner Cleaning and Maintenance for Branch Capture.
Note:	We recommend cleaning your scanner weekly to prevent poor quality images, scanner jams, and piggybacks. Follow the scanner manufacturerГўВҖВҷs cleaning and maintenance procedures.
Repair torn items with clear tape. Ensure the leading and lower edges are smooth. You can also use clear tape to smooth out wrinkles in an item that cause MICR or amount misreads.
Sort items in the deposit by small and large sizes. Put the larger items behind the smaller items in the scanner hopper. Put items in the same order as the tape listing.
If the scanner model doesnГўВҖВҷt have a built-in jogger, fan items to separate them, then tap the bundle to align the leading edges together.
7.14	Resolving Scanner Jams
Scanner jams occur when items get caught in the scannerГўВҖВҷs track or the scanner detects a double feed. If the scanner detects a jam or double feed, the following error message appears on the Deposit Item List page: ГўВҖВң
Transport Problem
: The hopper is jammedГўВҖВқ.
Resolve a scanner jam as follows:
Select
Recover
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Recovering a Jammed Deposit
Review the error message, then recover the deposit as follows. See Figure 2.
ГўВҲВ’
Remove all items from the scannerГўВҖВҷs track and the hopper. If an item is stuck in the scanner track, follow your scanner modelГўВҖВҷs instructions to access the document track.
ГўВҲВ’
Inspect the items for damage. Smooth out wrinkled items and repair with clear tape if necessary.
ГўВҲВ’
Organize the items and start the bundle with the item after the one displayed on screen. Put the items in the scanner hopper.
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Restart Scanner
to scan the remaining items.
Figure 2: Restarting a Jammed Deposit
Note:	If an item doesnГўВҖВҷt feed through the scanner, scan the item on its own or scan the cheque between two other cheques that have clean edges.
If the scanner jams every time that you process a deposit:
Clean the scanner. See Section 7.7, Scanner Cleaning and Maintenance for Branch Capture.
Note:	Clean scanners on a regular basis to prevent poor quality images, scanner jams, and piggybacks. We recommend cleaning your scanner weekly. Follow the scanner manufacturerГўВҖВҷs cleaning and maintenance procedures.
Log out of Branch Capture.
Unplug the scannerГўВҖВҷs power supply.
Unplug the scannerГўВҖВҷs USB cable from the computer.
Restart the computer.
Plug in the scannerГўВҖВҷs power supply.
Plug the scannerГўВҖВҷs USB cable into the computer.
Initialize the scanner as described in Section 7.4, Initializing and Testing the Scanner for Branch Capture.
Locate the deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Add the remaining items to the deposit or delete the deposit and rescan the items. See Section 9.13, Deleting a Branch Capture Deposit, and Section 9.5, Creating a Branch Capture Deposit, respectively for procedures.
Note: 	If the scanner continues to jam, it may be due to a hardware issue. Review your scanner manual or contact the scanner manufacturer or your scanner supplier for further instructions.
7.15	Replacing a Scanner with a New Scanner
If a scanner becomes inoperable and you must temporarily or permanently replace it, you can use the same scanner driver if the replacement scanner is the same model as the original scanner. If the replacement scanner is a different model, you must uninstall the current driver and download and install the appropriate driver.
Uninstall the current scanner driver as follows:
Enter ГўВҖВңadd or remove programsГўВҖВқ in your Windows Search Bar, then select
Control Panel
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Locating the Scanner Driver
Locate ГўВҖВңPassportWebClient
ГўВҖВқ
in the list of installed apps, select
More options
(three dots), then select
Uninstall
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Uninstalling the Scanner Driver
Select
Uninstall
to remove the scanner driver.
Ensure that the scanner is disconnected from the computer, then select
OK
to proceed with the uninstall wizard. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Disconnecting the Scanner
Select
Yes
to confirm. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Confirming Scanner USB is disconnected
Select
Finish
to complete the uninstall. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Confirming the Driver is Uninstalled
Install the replacement scanner driver as described in Section 7.3, Installing or Updating the Scanner Driver for Branch Capture.
Note:	If you replace a scanner during a billing month and are charged for both scanners, see Section 7.16, Investigating and Adjusting Scanner Billing Charges, for the procedures to request a billing adjustment. However, if your financial institution uses a backup scanner along with the primary scanner(s), the backup scanner is considered active, and there wonГўВҖВҷt be a billing adjustment.
7.16	Investigating and Adjusting Scanner Billing Charges
Scanner billing references the scanner serial number thatГўВҖВҷs recorded when items are scanned and transmitted for processing. If an active scanner is replaced by a backup scanner during the current billing month, both scanners will result in a billing charge. Contact your central for an investigation and billing charge adjustment.
Central 1 clients
ГўВҖВ“ Create a ServiceNow ticket and provide a copy of the billing report indicating which scanner was replaced during the billing month.
Non-Central 1 clients
ГўВҖВ“ Contact your provincial centralГўВҖВҷs support team.
Important
:	If your financial institution uses a backup scanner along with the primary scanner(s), the backup scanner is considered active and there wonГўВҖВҷt be a billing adjustment.
7.17	Shared Computers
Saved browser settings (e.g., cookies and/or temporary internet files) may prevent another user from logging in to Branch Capture on a shared computer.
Clear the cookies and/or temporary internet files and close the browser after completing deposits on shared computers. Refer to your browser provider for instructions.
END
